description: You are an edge-computing engineer about to push containerized workloads to several ARM-based IoT gateways. Before you can run the deployment CLI, it expects a very small ini-style configuration file in the current user’s home directory. Goal ----- 1. Create the directory /home/user/.iot if it does not yet exist. 2. Inside that directory create a text file named config.ini containing **exactly** the three lines shown below (no trailing spaces; the final line must end with a single newline character): [deployment] target=staging arch=arm64 Allowed tools ------------- • Any standard GNU/Linux shell utilities available in a fresh Ubuntu installation. • You only need one compound shell command to complete all steps (though you may of course issue more if you want). Verification the grader will perform ------------------------------------ • It will confirm that the file /home/user/.iot/config.ini exists. • It will read the file and compare it byte-for-byte against the following three-line, Unix-newline-terminated payload: [deployment]\n target=staging\n arch=arm64\n • It will check that the file is regular (not a directory or symlink) and world-readable (permission bits 644 or more permissive). • Directory /home/user/.iot must exist and be a directory. Expected console output ----------------------- After creating the file, print its absolute path followed by the word “OK” on the same line, exactly like this: /home/user/.iot/config.ini OK (That single line is the only console output the autograder will consider; supplemental output is allowed but will be ignored.)
truth:
Directory that must exist after task completion: /home/user/.iot  
    File that must be created: /home/user/.iot/config.ini  

    Exact required file contents (including final newline):  
    [deployment]\n  
    target=staging\n  
    arch=arm64\n  

    File type: regular text file  
    Minimal acceptable permissions: 0644  
    Grader will also accept more permissive bits (e.g., 0664, 0666).  

    Required agent console output (exact, single line, no extra spaces or newlines):  
    /home/user/.iot/config.ini OK
